jokes.text: You see, I am dizzle.
jokes.setup: Dave introducing himself formally to his audience
jokes.punchline: Using 'dizzle' (adding '-izzle' suffix popularized by Snoop Dogg) to his own name
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay that establishes Chappelle's playful persona. Economy is strong - maximum impact from minimal words.

jokes.text: I gotta get in on this 'being white' thing.
jokes.setup: Discussing corporate scandals where executives avoid jail time
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex systemic issues to simply needing to 'be white'
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary with perfect economy. Subverts expectations by making the absurd solution sound logical.

jokes.text: Snugglebunny! (screaming)
jokes.setup: White collar criminal having romantic moment with wife
jokes.punchline: Interrupted by violent police raid
jokes.explanation: Solid contrast between the intimate pet name and violent interruption. Good setup for the reversal premise.

jokes.text: Police burst in with excessive force, shooting the dog
jokes.setup: Corporate executive in peaceful domestic scene
jokes.punchline: Sudden violent police raid with gunshots and aggression
jokes.explanation: Effective contrast between white-collar and street-level police treatment. The shock value works.

jokes.text: Nigga, I said stop calling here, all right? I'm baggin' up the coke up as fast as I can!
jokes.setup: Phone rings in drug dealer's house
jokes.punchline: Answering phone while openly discussing cocaine trafficking, not knowing it's police
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - audience knows it's police while character incriminates himself.

jokes.text: I've got some trim coming at 12:00
jokes.setup: Scheduling arrest around personal activities
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning sex appointment to police officer
jokes.explanation: Adds to the absurdity with crude humor that feels character-appropriate.

jokes.text: One love. Uh, yeah... I love you, too.
jokes.setup: Drug dealer ends call with 'One love' greeting
jokes.punchline: White detective awkwardly interpreting it literally and reciprocating
jokes.explanation: Perfect cultural miscommunication joke with excellent economy and character work.

jokes.text: Would you do me a favor and not smoke, I'm allergic.
jokes.setup: Corporate executive being interrogated
jokes.punchline: Making polite requests about smoking while being aggressively questioned for fraud
jokes.explanation: Shows entitlement and privilege through mundane complaint during serious interrogation.

jokes.text: Somebody take a piss in here? It was me! I peed...
jokes.setup: Someone asking about urine smell in interrogation room
jokes.punchline: Executive immediately and pathetically admitting he urinated himself
jokes.explanation: Standard humiliation humor that serves the premise but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: I would like to complement you, gentlemen on a very classy baller-ass spread with cheeses that I've never even seen before.
jokes.setup: Drug dealer at his sentencing hearing
jokes.punchline: Complimenting the fancy courthouse refreshments like he's at a party
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing how different treatment creates different attitudes toward legal proceedings.

jokes.text: I got caught up with some poonany.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he's late to his own sentencing
jokes.punchline: Crude admission about sexual encounter to court officials
jokes.explanation: Reinforces character's casual attitude but relies on crude humor.

jokes.text: Oh, I know, man, on Thanksgiving I be passing turkeys out like 'Nino Brown,' baby.
jokes.setup: Judge mentioning he's done good for the community
jokes.punchline: Comparing his charity work to a notorious drug dealer from 'New Jack City'
jokes.explanation: Clever reference that shows character's obliviousness to how his comparison undermines his point.

jokes.text: When I get out, can I still traffic rocks to the community?
jokes.setup: Judge giving lenient sentence to drug dealer
jokes.punchline: Dealer asking if he can continue dealing drugs after his light sentence
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist moment that encapsulates the sketch's premise about lenient treatment.

jokes.text: Heh, heh, heh... jail's the shit!
jokes.setup: Drug dealer learning about his cushy prison sentence
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastically endorsing jail time like it's a vacation
jokes.explanation: Strong character reaction that reinforces the central premise about privilege.

jokes.text: Then his wife threw her titties in my hand. It was weird, your honor.
jokes.setup: Detective giving testimony about arresting corporate executive
jokes.punchline: Bizarre claim about assault delivered matter-of-factly, followed by understated reaction
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation with perfect deadpan delivery. 'It was weird' is magnificent understatement.

jokes.text: You're an animal. A filthy, big-lipped beast!
jokes.setup: Judge sentencing the drug dealer
jokes.punchline: Racist language from the supposedly impartial judge
jokes.explanation: Effective satirical commentary on judicial bias, though the racism makes it more provocative than funny.

jokes.text: Plenty of time to lift weights and convert to Islam.
jokes.setup: Judge giving life sentence to drug dealer
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning prison stereotypes as if they're scheduled activities
jokes.explanation: Commentary on prison stereotypes but feels somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Um, no, but I can tell you that I plead the fizzith.
jokes.setup: Reporter asking about drug cartels
jokes.punchline: Adding 'izzle' suffix to 'fifth' while pleading the Fifth Amendment
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that maintains character voice while referencing legal knowledge.

jokes.text: There... are... I said there are so many amendments in the Constitution of the United States of America I can only choose one
jokes.setup: Reporter continuing to ask questions
jokes.punchline: Dramatic buildup treating constitutional amendments like a difficult choice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that treats legal procedure like an impossible decision. Great dramatic tension.

jokes.text: One, two, three, four... fifth! Anything you say... fifth!
jokes.setup: Character's dramatic constitutional speech
jokes.punchline: Counting to five like a child, then compulsively saying 'fifth' to everything
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the buildup. Counting to five is childlike and the repetition becomes compulsive.

jokes.text: Sometimes, the endless pursuit of crack leaves me tired and depleted.
jokes.setup: Crack addict in energy drink commercial
jokes.punchline: Describing crack addiction like everyday fatigue that needs an energy boost
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tyrone Biggums character work. Treating addiction as a minor inconvenience is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: But now cocaine comes in a delicious shake! 'Red Balls!' It gives me wings!
jokes.setup: Energy drink commercial format
jokes.punchline: Advertising cocaine as an energy drink with Red Bull's slogan
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody that combines commercial format with drug addiction. The Red Bull reference is perfect.

jokes.text: My baby! I left my baby in the car!
jokes.setup: Tyrone enjoying his cocaine energy drink
jokes.punchline: Sudden realization he abandoned his child while getting high
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective character moment. The sudden shift from euphoria to panic is well-executed.

jokes.text: FYI, people still do steal radios, hon. How do you think I got this suit?
jokes.setup: Tyrone in a nice suit after superhuman feats
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting the suit is stolen while correcting misconceptions about radio theft
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency. The casual admission and outdated reference work well.

jokes.text: Might I have a Red Balls, please?
jokes.setup: Tyrone in jail
jokes.punchline: Politely asking jailer for cocaine energy drink like it's a normal request
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the commercial with appropriate character voice. The politeness contrasts well with the request.

jokes.text: Might I have a Red Balls, please?
jokes.setup: Tyrone is in jail
jokes.punchline: Politely requests the cocaine energy drink from his jailer
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tyrone's unshakeable addiction and politeness even in jail.

jokes.text: I can get some money for this.
jokes.setup: Tyrone holding empty Red Balls can
jokes.punchline: Immediately thinking about recycling the can for drug money
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment. Even after supernatural experiences, he's focused on recycling for drug money.

jokes.text: Yes, Arsenio Hall will get another show. It will be called, 'Good Morning Black America.' It will be shown at noon throughout the country.
jokes.setup: Question about whether Arsenio Hall will get another show
jokes.punchline: Show called 'Good Morning' that airs at noon, highlighting how black programming gets poor time slots
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on media representation wrapped in prophecy format. The contradiction is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Arnold Schwarzenegger will divorce his wife and marry Shirley Temple Black. And she will be, Mrs. Shirley Temple Black-negger.
jokes.setup: Question from French caller (though no question is audible)
jokes.punchline: Creating surname 'Black-negger' through marriage prediction
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay setup, though it relies on racial humor for impact. The construction is well-done.

jokes.text: White people love Wayne Brady because he makes... Bryant Gumbel look like Malcolm X.
jokes.setup: Question about why white people love Wayne Brady
jokes.punchline: Comparison showing how non-threatening he is by making even the mild Gumbel seem radical
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comparative structure with perfect economy. The progression from Brady to Gumbel to Malcolm X is masterful.

jokes.text: If you wearin' baggy shit now and you actin' hard, if you from LA You motherfuckers was wearing some strange shit.
jokes.setup: Talking about 1980s fashion trends
jokes.punchline: Calling out current hard guys for their past fashion choices
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the Prince story. Charlie's storytelling voice establishes credibility through shared experience.

jokes.text: Prince had on, like, a... it was like a zorro-type outfit. It had the ruffles come down the front. He had the big perm fluffed out and all that. And the mustache, you know, just drawn on his face. And it looked like something that a figure skater would wear.
jokes.setup: Describing Prince's appearance at the club
jokes.punchline: Comparing his flamboyant outfit to figure skating costume
jokes.explanation: Vivid description that builds the visual comedy. The figure skater comparison is particularly effective.

jokes.text: Prince had on, like, a... it was like a zorro-type outfit. It had the ruffles come down the front.
jokes.setup: Charlie describing Prince's club outfit
jokes.punchline: Detailed description of flamboyant costume emphasizing femininity
jokes.explanation: Sets up visual comedy. Charlie's descriptive storytelling style building the scene.

jokes.text: I heard mad cats, like, 'yo, Shalamar got this new girl in there. Man, that bitch fine like a motherfucker.' They was talkin' about Mickey Free, okay? Mickey Free is not a girl.
jokes.setup: People talking about attractive new Shalamar member
jokes.punchline: Reveal that they were talking about a man
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke that establishes the era's androgynous aesthetic. The reveal works well.

jokes.text: Hello, Eddie Murphy. Prince, what's up? I'm a big fan of your comedies.
jokes.setup: Prince meeting Eddie Murphy at the club
jokes.punchline: Prince's overly formal greeting contrasted with Eddie's casual response
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Prince's formality versus street casualness. Sets up their dynamic.

jokes.text: Hello, Eddie Murphy. Prince, what's up? I'm a big fan of your comedies. Ooh, that's hot, Prince.
jokes.setup: Prince approaching Eddie at the club
jokes.punchline: Prince's formal greeting and Eddie's stereotypical response
jokes.explanation: Sets up character dynamics. Prince's formality and Eddie's casual response establish their personalities.

jokes.text: Fruity, get the car. Assemble your crew, I'll be outside.
jokes.setup: Prince inviting Eddie and crew to his house
jokes.punchline: Calling his assistant 'Fruity' while giving formal commands
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment. 'Fruity' as a name and the formal delivery work well together.

jokes.text: This bores me. Is anyone up for a game of basketball?
jokes.setup: Everyone enjoying music at Prince's house
jokes.punchline: Prince dismissing his own music to suggest basketball
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the legendary basketball sequence. The casual dismissal of music for sports is brilliantly unexpected.

jokes.text: This bores me. Is anyone up for a game of basketball?
jokes.setup: Everyone listening to music at Prince's house
jokes.punchline: Prince, in full costume, suggests basketball
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the legendary basketball scene. Absurd suggestion delivered with Prince's characteristic directness.

jokes.text: How about you and your friends versus me and The Revolution.
jokes.setup: Prince suggesting basketball
jokes.punchline: Formal challenge using his band name 'The Revolution' for a pickup basketball team
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that treats his band as a basketball team. The formality makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: How about you and your friends versus me and The Revolution.
jokes.setup: Prince suggesting basketball game
jokes.punchline: Formal challenge involving his band 'The Revolution'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation. Using band name in sports context shows Prince's grandiosity.

jokes.text: They still got on the same shit they was wearin' at the club.
jokes.setup: Expectation that they'd change into basketball clothes
jokes.punchline: Prince's crew playing basketball in club outfits
jokes.explanation: Great visual setup that subverts expectations. Playing basketball in club clothes adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: The shirts against the blouses.
jokes.setup: Need to distinguish teams for basketball game
jokes.punchline: Calling Prince's team 'blouses' instead of traditional shirts vs skins
jokes.explanation: Legendary line with perfect economy. 'Blouses' becomes an iconic insult while being technically accurate.

jokes.text: what are you angry about? I mean, you know where you got that shirt from and it damn sure wasn't the men's department.
jokes.setup: Prince looking angry after the blouses comment
jokes.punchline: Charlie doubling down by pointing out where Prince actually shops
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that doesn't back down. The men's department line is perfectly constructed.

jokes.text: He was getting rebounds like Charles Barkley. Snatchin' it down!
jokes.setup: Prince showing basketball skills
jokes.punchline: Comparison to NBA superstar Charles Barkley
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Prince's abilities. Charlie's amazement comes through clearly.

jokes.text: This cat could ball, man. He was crossing cats, like ice. Crossed me up. Made my knees slam together.
jokes.setup: Expectation that Prince would be terrible at basketball
jokes.punchline: Prince being surprisingly skilled, with vivid description of Charlie's humiliation
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with great physical comedy description. 'Made my knees slam together' is vivid and humiliating.

jokes.text: Let's run the play, computer blue. Garland picky.
jokes.setup: Prince calling plays during basketball game
jokes.punchline: Using song titles as basketball plays
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Prince's artistic mind applied to sports. Using song titles as plays is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: They was kinda settin' these fruity picks, man... you don't really wanna be bent over in front of a cat like that
jokes.setup: Describing Prince's team setting basketball picks
jokes.punchline: Implication of inappropriate positioning during picks
jokes.explanation: Relies on homophobic humor for impact. The physical comedy aspect works but the premise is dated.

jokes.text: Why don't you purify yourself in the waters of lake Minnetonka.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking for a towel after the game
jokes.punchline: Prince giving mystical non-answer instead of practical help
jokes.explanation: Legendary Prince line that perfectly captures his mystical persona. The incongruity with the request is perfect.

jokes.text: Good hustle. (slap) Yo, man, I'm not on your team.
jokes.setup: Prince congratulating someone after the game
jokes.punchline: Slaps Charlie's butt thinking he's on his team
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment. Shows Prince's obliviousness and adds to Charlie's humiliation.

jokes.text: Good hustle. (slap) Yo, man, I'm not on your team.
jokes.setup: Post-game congratulations
jokes.punchline: Prince slapping opponent's ass, immediate awkward correction
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with immediate payoff. Charlie's correction is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Game... blouses.
jokes.setup: Prince's team winning decisively
jokes.punchline: Prince delivering victory declaration using Charlie's insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that reclaims the insult as victory declaration. The pause before 'blouses' is masterful.

jokes.text: After it was all over, he took us in the house and served us pancakes.
jokes.setup: Expectation of what happens after competitive basketball game
jokes.punchline: Prince serving breakfast food to his opponents
jokes.explanation: Legendary ending that perfectly caps the absurdist escalation. Pancakes as post-basketball meal is brilliantly random.

jokes.text: Do you guys want some grapes?
jokes.setup: Prince hosting after basketball victory
jokes.punchline: Offering grapes like a fancy host after serving pancakes
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that maintains Prince's bizarre hosting style. Grapes after pancakes adds to the surreal domesticity.

jokes.text: Bitches.
jokes.setup: Prince being polite host offering refreshments
jokes.punchline: Sudden dismissive comment under his breath
jokes.explanation: Perfect button to the story. One word that completely subverts Prince's polite hosting persona.

jokes.text: I'm rich, biatch!
jokes.setup: Show closing
jokes.punchline: Dave's signature catchphrase delivered with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Classic Chappelle catchphrase that became culturally iconic. Simple but effective character moment.
